name: Hook_Mech

infobox.Systems: Grappling Hook(Brute)EffectUse a grapple to pull Mech towards objects, or units to the Mech.Upgrade IShield Ally ()If used on an ally unit it gives them a Shield.
infobox.Special: Natural Armor: Weapon damage to this unit is reduced by 1. All other damage (Push, Blocking, Fire, etc.) is unaffected.

sections.Team: The mech is available as part of theBlitzkriegsquad.
sections.Strategy:
In its default Blitzkrieg squad, try using the Hook Mech as a conductor to make theLightning Mechhit more enemies; it will only take 1 damage from a baseElectric Whipin the early game, thanks to having natural Armor.
The Grappling Hook will pull movable objects (other mechs,Vekor rocks) towards it, and will pull itself towards immovable objects (such asCivilian Buildings,Mountainsor objects and enemies that areStable). TheShield Allyupgrade can be situationally useful if looking to extend the Electric Whip whip chain with an otherwise frail unit, but is not as important to get as additional movement and HP; movement will help it get the most out of Grappling Hook and position itself to create longer chains, while additional HP will be necessary for it to endure more hits when the Electric Whip weapon is upgraded.
The Hook Mech has no direct damage output, so consider purchasing a damagingweaponfor it. Alternatively, consider lettingKazaaakplethpilot the Hook Mech, to provide it with an attack immediately through his ability. Another good pilot choice isProspero, as he can let the Hook Mech fly, allowing for better positioning and greater control.
The Hook Mech is more limited inCustom Squadswithout the Lightning Mech present, but it can still use the natural armor to trade Grappling Hook with theThruster Mech. Since they are both Brute class Mechs, it costs no reactor cores to swap and equipReverse Thrusters, while the Thruster Mech uses Grappling Hook to greater effect with its superior mobility letting pull enemies into hazardous tiles more easily.

In its default Blitzkrieg squad, try using the Hook Mech as a conductor to make the
Lightning Mech
hit more enemies; it will only take 1 damage from a base
Electric Whip
in the early game, thanks to having natural Armor.
The Grappling Hook will pull movable objects (other mechs,
Vek
or rocks) towards it, and will pull itself towards immovable objects (such as
Civilian Buildings
,
Mountains
or objects and enemies that are
Stable
). The
Shield Ally
upgrade can be situationally useful if looking to extend the Electric Whip whip chain with an otherwise frail unit, but is not as important to get as additional movement and HP; movement will help it get the most out of Grappling Hook and position itself to create longer chains, while additional HP will be necessary for it to endure more hits when the Electric Whip weapon is upgraded.
The Hook Mech has no direct damage output, so consider purchasing a damaging
weapon
for it. Alternatively, consider letting
Kazaaakpleth
pilot the Hook Mech, to provide it with an attack immediately through his ability. Another good pilot choice is
Prospero
, as he can let the Hook Mech fly, allowing for better positioning and greater control.
The Hook Mech is more limited in
Custom Squads
without the Lightning Mech present, but it can still use the natural armor to trade Grappling Hook with the
Thruster Mech
. Since they are both Brute class Mechs, it costs no reactor cores to swap and equip
Reverse Thrusters
, while the Thruster Mech uses Grappling Hook to greater effect with its superior mobility letting pull enemies into hazardous tiles more easily.
